Port `fcp-host` runtime entrypoint, orchestration loops, and process/task supervision from Tokio to asupersync-first execution.

## Scope
- Host startup/shutdown lifecycle, background workers, request fanout, control-plane async paths.
- Replace Tokio-specific sync/time/process usage with substrate-approved primitives.

## Rationale
Host is the gateway coordinator; runtime instability here cascades across all connector interactions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Host binary starts, serves, and drains with no Tokio runtime dependencies.
- Graceful shutdown is deterministic under load/cancellation tests.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-235t.9","title":"[ASUPERSYNC] Migrate fcp-streaming WebSocket/Reconnect/Backpressure Engine","description":"# Goal
Rebuild streaming transport loops on asupersync websocket/network primitives with explicit backpressure contracts.

## Scope
- WebSocket client/server loops, reconnect strategy, stream buffering, cancellation-safe drains.
- Replace tokio-tungstenite and Tokio-driven coordination where feasible.

## Rationale
Streaming paths are high-churn and failure-prone. This migration must preserve liveness while strengthening overload behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reconnect logic, buffer limits, and timeout behavior are deterministic in integration tests.
- No unbounded queue growth under burst scenarios.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-24bc","title":"Policy Bundles: Signed ZonePolicy Packs + Diff/Preview/Rollback","description":"# Policy Bundles: Signed ZonePolicy Packs + Diff/Preview/Rollback

## Goal
Provide a safe, versioned workflow to propose, review, preview, apply, and roll back policy changes across zones.

## Why (User Impact)
- Prevents accidental policy regressions and privilege creep.
- Enables deterministic review with clear diffs and risk summaries.
- Makes policy changes auditable and reversible with one command.

## Scope
- PolicyBundle object (signed, versioned, immutable).
- Deterministic diff and risk summary engine.
- Preview evaluator (would-allow/deny deltas).
- CLI workflow for create/diff/preview/apply/rollback.

## Non-goals
- UI/editor; CLI only for now.

## Deliverables
- Canonical schema + signature rules for PolicyBundle.
- Diff + risk summary outputs with stable ordering.
- Preview evaluation reports with reason codes.
- CLI commands with JSON/human outputs.
- Unit/integration tests + E2E script with structured logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Policy bundles are signed and verifiable; invalid signatures fail closed.
- Preview explains allow/deny deltas before apply.
- Rollback to previous bundle is one command and auditable.","notes":"Progress (WhiteDesert, 2026-02-02): Added fcp policy bundle diff CLI plumbing in crates/fcp-cli/src/policy/mod.rs. Command accepts --before/--after bundle JSON + --objects-before/--objects-after object maps (object_id -> policy object), resolves to PolicyBundleResolved, then uses fcp_core::diff_policy_bundles for deterministic diff + risk summary. Also fixed clippy doc issues in policy bundle schema test.

- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-2zy9","title":"[IDEA-01] SDK Connector Runtime Kit (Supervised Streaming/Polling)","description":"# SDK Connector Runtime Kit (Supervised Streaming/Polling)

## Goal
Provide a reusable, SDK-level runtime kit for connector authors that encapsulates the reliability patterns from the Discord/Telegram studies: supervised loops, backoff, resume, heartbeat/zombie detection, deterministic shutdown, and structured health transitions.

## Background / Why
Discord (streaming WebSocket) and Telegram (polling) independently implement the same reliability machinery. Without a shared SDK surface we get duplicated bugs, inconsistent health semantics, and regressions across connectors. A runtime kit makes these patterns mechanical and accelerates new connector development.

## Scope
- SDK module(s) used by connector implementations without host changes.
- No changes to external APIs; this is internal SDK ergonomics and correctness.
- Includes testing utilities and reference examples.

## Deliverables
- `fcp_sdk::runtime::{StreamingSupervisor, PollingSupervisor, SupervisorConfig}` with:
  - exponential backoff + jitter
  - retry budgets + cooldowns
  - shutdown signals
  - session resume hooks
  - deterministic health transitions (Healthy/Degraded/Unhealthy)
- Connector-facing trait adapters for platform-specific state (session IDs, cursor offsets).
- Structured logging fields (`connector_id`, `zone_id`, `operation_id`, `stream_key`, `sequence`).

## Tests
- Unit tests for backoff, retry budgets, and shutdown transitions.
- Integration tests with a fake stream/poll source (no external calls).
- Deterministic logging snapshots (JSONL schema once flywheel_connectors-11g2 lands).

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least one streaming connector can replace its custom loop with the SDK supervisor.
- At least one polling connector can replace its custom loop with the SDK supervisor.
- Health transitions are deterministic and observable via logs.","notes":"All child tasks complete/blocked (flywheel_connectors-fgot, flywheel_connectors-uhmy, flywheel_connectors-3zhv closed; flywheel_connectors-19rr completed and blocked). Waiting on dependency flywheel_connectors-1n78.20 to close.

[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add spectral routing score (effective resistance / Laplacian) as optional metric and log chosen path.
- Set gossip TTL from measured mixing-time; add convergence test that asserts bound.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use SmallVec for usually-small object/symbol lists to reduce allocations.
- Batch symbol requests and use Bytes for zero-copy in hot paths.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-308","title":"fcp.sentry: Performance monitoring data access","description":"# fcp.sentry: Performance monitoring data access (FCP2)

## Goal
Expose Sentry performance/trace surfaces so agents can:
- identify slow transactions and regressions
- correlate errors with performance degradation
- fetch trace/transaction summaries for RCA

## Scope
### Read operations
- Query transactions by project/environment/time range.
- Fetch a transaction/trace summary:
  - duration
  - spans breakdown
  - key tags
  - sample trace ids
- Fetch performance metrics trends (where available).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `sentry.performance.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Performance data may include URLs, user agents, and other sensitive metadata:
  - treat as tainted
  - avoid logging raw payloads

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `sentry.performance.transactions.query`
  - `sentry.performance.transaction.get`
  - `sentry.performance.trace.get_summary`
- Pagination helpers + time-window query helpers.

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - query construction and pagination
  - normalization of key metrics
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff
  - taint propagation

## Acceptance criteria
- Agents can retrieve stable, comparable performance summaries for RCA.
- No secrets/PII leak through logs.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-3091d","title":"[AUDIT][HIGH] fcp-mesh degraded control-plane accepts tampered symbol payloads without AEAD verification","description":"P1 targeted audit. Evidence: crates/fcp-mesh/src/degraded.rs:208-218 encodes CONTROL_PLANE SymbolRecord entries with plaintext data and placeholder zero auth_tag values; crates/fcp-mesh/src/degraded.rs:580-587 then feeds symbol.data directly into RaptorQDecoder with only a comment saying auth_tag verification would happen in production; crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:1843-1849 routes raw FcpsFrame input into degraded_decoder.process_frame without any per-symbol integrity check at the MeshNode boundary. Repro: encode a ControlPlaneEnvelope with DegradedModeEncoder::encode, flip one byte in frame.symbols[0].data (leave auth_tag unchanged), then call MeshNode::decode_control_plane or DegradedModeDecoder::process_frame; the tampered symbol is still admitted to reconstruction instead of being rejected as forged. Impact: any caller path that reaches degraded CONTROL_PLANE decoding before a lower transport layer has authenticated symbol contents can inject or corrupt control-plane payloads despite ENCRYPTED/CONTROL_PLANE flags. Suggested fix: wire zone-key-based SymbolEnvelope decrypt/auth_tag verification into degraded decode before decoder.add_symbol, and fail closed until authenticated symbol decryption is available rather than accepting placeholder tags.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T16:31:44.621497Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T16:31:44.621497Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-30bf","title":"[IDEA-07] Safe Formatting Helpers (HTML/Markdown → Plaintext Fallback)","description":"# Safe Formatting Helpers (HTML/Markdown → Plaintext Fallback)

## Goal
Provide SDK utilities for safe message formatting that automatically fall back to plaintext when the target platform rejects markup.

## Background / Why
Telegram in particular rejects malformed HTML entities; a robust fallback prevents message loss. This pattern is reusable for other platforms with strict markup parsers.

## Scope
- SDK helper that attempts formatting and falls back when parse errors are detected.
- Connector-facing API that can plug into send_message operations.

## Deliverables
- `Formatter` helper with `render_with_fallback()`.
- Error classifier for parse/format errors.
- Example usage in Telegram-style connector.

## Tests
- Unit tests for fallback behavior.
- Integration test that simulates parse failure and verifies fallback send.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Formatting failures never drop messages; they fallback to plaintext.
- Errors are logged without leaking message content.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]

- Use sequential test budgets with e-value thresholds to stop early when safe.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Parallelize vector generation with deterministic seeds.
- Cache canonical forms to avoid repeated parse cost.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ssb","title":"fcp.homeassistant: Integration aggregation layer for 2000+ device types","description":"# fcp.homeassistant: Integration aggregation layer for 2000+ device types (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide a unified, typed abstraction over Home Assistant’s entity/service model so the connector can support thousands of device integrations without bespoke code for each.

## Scope
- Entity registry discovery:
  - list entities with domain/area/device metadata
  - expose stable identifiers
- Domain capability mapping:
  - map entity domains to capability families (read vs write vs security-adjacent)
- Normalized “device control” schema:
  - a small set of generic operations covers most integrations

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `ha.entities.read`
- `ha.entities.write` (dangerous-ish)
- `ha.services.call` (dangerous-ish)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to configured HA host.
- Strict safety for security-adjacent domains (locks, alarms) must be policy-owned and approval gated.

## Deliverables
- Canonical types:
  - `HaEntityRef`, `HaEntityState`, `HaServiceCall`
- Operation schemas:
  - `ha.entity.list`
  - `ha.entity.get`
  - `ha.entity.capabilities.get`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - entity registry parsing
  - domain mapping correctness
  - redaction of sensitive attributes

## Acceptance criteria
- The connector can support new HA integrations without adding new per-device code paths.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-sy4","title":"fcp.sentry: Auto-create Beads from Sentry issues","description":"# fcp.sentry - Error Tracking & Performance Monitoring Connector (FCP2)

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 85/100** — Important Tier 2 connector (Observability family).

**Why Critical**: Sentry is the **leading application error tracking platform**. Enables agents to monitor application health, diagnose errors, and automatically create actionable work items from production issues. Essential for autonomous debugging workflows.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Strategic Investment (High Value + Debugging Automation)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 2 Productivity (weeks 5-8)

---

## V2 Specification Compliance (NORMATIVE)

### ConnectorArchetype
```rust
archetypes: vec\![
    ConnectorArchetype::Operational,  // Issue management, alerts
    ConnectorArchetype::Streaming,    // Webhook events
]
```
- **Operational**: Issue CRUD, alert configuration, release tracking
- **Streaming**: Webhooks for new issues, regressions, alerts

### ConnectorStateModel
```rust
state_model: ConnectorStateModel::SingletonWriter
```
- **SingletonWriter**: Webhook deduplication, issue sync state
- State tracked: `{ processed_events: HashSet<EventId>, sync_cursors: HashMap<ProjectId, Cursor> }`
- Lease required for writer fencing

### ConnectorFormat
```rust
format: ConnectorFormat::Wasi
```
- **WASI recommended**: Handles API tokens
- Memory isolation for credential protection
- Capability-gated hostcalls

### NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)
```rust
NetworkConstraints {
    // Sentry API endpoints
    host_allow: vec\![
        "sentry.io".into(),
        "*.sentry.io".into(),
        // Self-hosted: explicitly configured
    ],
    port_allow: vec\![443],
    deny_localhost: true,
    deny_private_ranges: true,  // Override for self-hosted
    deny_tailnet_ranges: true,
    deny_ip_literals: true,
    require_sni: true,
    require_host_canonicalization: true,
}
```

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
```rust
// Stack traces may contain sensitive data
stack_trace.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT;

// User context in errors is PII
user_context.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::USER_SUPPLIED;

// Webhook payloads are external
webhook_event.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT;

// URLs in breadcrumbs are unverified
if breadcrumb.contains_urls() {
    breadcrumb.provenance.taint |= TaintFlags::UNVERIFIED_LINK;
}
```

### SafetyTier per Operation
| Operation | SafetyTier | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| `sentry.issues.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `sentry.events.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `sentry.projects.read` | Safe | Metadata |
| `sentry.issues.update` | Risky | Status changes |
| `sentry.issues.assign` | Risky | Routing changes |
| `sentry.alerts.manage` | Dangerous | Alert configuration |
| `sentry.issues.delete` | Dangerous | Irreversible |
| `sentry.projects.admin` | Dangerous | Project settings |

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Issue Management
- List and search issues
- View event details and stack traces
- Update issue status (resolve, ignore, unresolve)
- Assign issues to team members
- Add tags and annotations

### Event Analysis
- Inspect error events
- View breadcrumbs and context
- Analyze stack traces
- Correlate with releases

### Alerting & Monitoring
- Configure alert rules
- Manage notification channels
- View alert history

### Beads Integration
- Auto-create Beads from Sentry issues
- Sync issue status
- Link Beads to releases

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Show me the top errors this week"
"Resolve all issues fixed in this release"
"Create a Bead for this critical error"
"Find errors related to this stack trace"
"Set up an alert for high error rates"
"Assign this issue to the backend team"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### GitHub Integration
**Error-to-code linking:**
- Stack trace → source code
- Issue → PR reference
- Release correlation

### Linear/Jira Integration
- Error → issue creation
- Status sync
- Triage workflows

### Slack Integration
- Alert notifications
- Issue escalation

### Datadog/Grafana Integration
- Error rate metrics
- Performance correlation
- Unified observability

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **REST API**: Primary CRUD operations
- **Webhooks**: Event notifications

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `serde_json` — JSON handling

### Authentication
| Mode | Use Case |
|------|----------|
| Auth Token | User-based access |
| DSN | Event ingestion (not for connector) |
| Internal Integration | Org-wide access |

### Rate Limits
```rust
RateLimits {
    requests_per_second: 10,
    // Concurrent request limits apply
}
```

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:ops:sentry:full` | Full access | All projects |
| `z:ops:sentry:project:<slug>` | Project-scoped | Single project |
| `z:ops:sentry:readonly` | Read-only | Safe operations |

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `operational` — Issue management, alerts
- `streaming` — Webhook events

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `sentry.io:443`
- `*.sentry.io:443`
- Self-hosted: configured hostname

### Credential Injection
- Auth tokens via egress proxy
- Webhook secrets for validation

---

## Capability Model

### Read Operations
- `sentry.issues.read` — List/get issues
- `sentry.events.read` — Event details
- `sentry.projects.read` — Project info

### Write Operations
- `sentry.issues.update` — Status changes
- `sentry.issues.assign` — Assignment

### Admin Operations
- `sentry.alerts.manage` — Alert rules (Dangerous)
- `sentry.issues.delete` — Delete issues (Dangerous)
- `sentry.projects.admin` — Project settings (Dangerous)

---

### List Issues
```
GET /api/0/projects/{org_slug}/{project_slug}/issues/
```

### Get Issue Details
```
GET /api/0/issues/{issue_id}/
```

### Update Issue Status
```json
PUT /api/0/issues/{issue_id}/
{
  "status": "resolved"
}
```

### List Events for Issue
```
GET /api/0/issues/{issue_id}/events/
```

---

## Beads Auto-Creation

### Trigger Sources
- New high-severity issue
- Issue regression
- Alert triggered
- Elevated error rate on release

### Mapping
```
Bead title: Sentry issue title (tainted)
Bead body: sanitized summary + links
Labels: project/environment/severity
```

### Deduplication
- Same Sentry issue ID → single Bead
- Idempotent webhook handling

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Request construction
- Response parsing
- Webhook signature validation
- Issue status mapping

### Integration Tests
- Mock Sentry API responses
- Beads creation workflow
- Error handling

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints
- Dangerous operation approval flow
- Audit logging

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real Sentry API calls in unit tests
- Issue listing and details work
- Status updates work
- Webhook handling functional
- Beads auto-creation works

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-t690","title":"TEST: fcp-host Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-host Unit Test Suite

## Goal
Validate `fcp-host` core behaviors (supervision, sandboxing, policy enforcement, logs) with deterministic tests and mocks.

## Scope
Unit tests for the Gateway/Orchestrator implementation.

## Test Categories

### Connector Supervisor
- [ ] Connector process spawning
- [ ] Process termination handling
- [ ] Health monitoring (heartbeat success/failure)
- [ ] Restart policy enforcement (max_restarts, backoff)
- [ ] Resource limit enforcement (memory, CPU)
- [ ] Crash detection and recovery
- [ ] Graceful shutdown signaling

#### Golden Vectors
```rust
// tests/vectors/host/supervisor_restart_policy.json
{
  "policy": {
    "max_restarts": 3,
    "backoff_base_ms": 100,
    "backoff_max_ms": 10000,
    "window_seconds": 60
  },
  "crashes": [
    {"at_ms": 0, "restart": true, "delay_ms": 100},
    {"at_ms": 200, "restart": true, "delay_ms": 200},
    {"at_ms": 600, "restart": true, "delay_ms": 400},
    {"at_ms": 1500, "restart": false, "reason": "max_restarts_exceeded"}
  ]
}
```

### IPC Hub
- [ ] Message routing correctness
- [ ] Request/response correlation
- [ ] Timeout handling (configurable per operation)
- [ ] Event fan-out to multiple subscribers
- [ ] Connection lifecycle (connect, disconnect, reconnect)
- [ ] Backpressure handling

#### IPC Test Scenarios
```rust
#[test]
fn test_request_response_correlation() {
    // Concurrent requests must be correlated correctly
    let hub = IpcHub::new();
    let req1 = hub.send_request("connector-a", Request { id: 1, ... }).await;
    let req2 = hub.send_request("connector-a", Request { id: 2, ... }).await;
    
    // Responses can arrive out of order
    hub.receive_response(Response { id: 2, ... });
    hub.receive_response(Response { id: 1, ... });
    
    // Each request gets correct response
    assert_eq\!(req1.await?.id, 1);
    assert_eq\!(req2.await?.id, 2);
}

#[test]
fn test_timeout_handling() {
    let hub = IpcHub::new_with_timeout(Duration::from_millis(100));
    let req = hub.send_request("connector-a", Request { ... });
    
    // No response within timeout
    let err = req.await.unwrap_err();
    assert\!(matches\!(err, IpcError::Timeout { operation_id, .. }));
}
```

### Zone Enforcer
- [ ] Zone configuration loading from ZoneDefinition
- [ ] Request zone validation (single-zone binding)
- [ ] Cross-zone policy enforcement (default deny)
- [ ] Data flow control (taint labels)
- [ ] Zone membership verification

### Capability Verifier
- [ ] COSE_Sign1 token parsing
- [ ] Ed25519 signature verification
- [ ] Capability scope checking (operation, connector)
- [ ] Constraint enforcement (rate limits, resource limits)
- [ ] Expiration handling (valid_from, valid_until)
- [ ] Grant object ID subset verification
- [ ] Revocation check integration

#### Capability Test Scenarios
```rust
// tests/vectors/host/capability_verification.json
[
  {
    "name": "valid_token",
    "token_hex": "d28443...",
    "expected": {"valid": true, "operations": ["invoke"]}
  },
  {
    "name": "expired_token",
    "token_hex": "d28443...",
    "expected": {"valid": false, "error": "FCP-2102"}
  },
  {
    "name": "invalid_signature",
    "token_hex": "d28443...",
    "expected": {"valid": false, "error": "FCP-2104"}
  },
  {
    "name": "revoked_grant",
    "token_hex": "d28443...",
    "revocation_seq": 42,
    "expected": {"valid": false, "error": "FCP-2105"}
  }
]
```

### Sandbox Manager
- [ ] Filesystem isolation setup (allowed paths, read-only mounts)
- [ ] Network filtering rules (NetworkConstraints)
- [ ] Configuration generation (TOML/JSON for jail)
- [ ] Resource cgroup configuration
- [ ] Credential injection (sealed secrets)

#### Sandbox Test Scenarios
```rust
#[test]
fn test_filesystem_isolation() {
    let sandbox = SandboxManager::new();
    let config = sandbox.configure(&ConnectorManifest {
        network_constraints: NetworkConstraints::Moderate,
        filesystem_access: vec\!["/data/connector"],
        ...
    });
    
    assert\!(config.allowed_paths.contains("/data/connector"));
    assert\!(\!config.allowed_paths.contains("/etc/passwd"));
    assert\!(config.read_only_paths.contains("/usr"));
}

#[test]
fn test_network_filtering_strict() {
    let config = sandbox.configure(&ConnectorManifest {
        network_constraints: NetworkConstraints::Strict,
        ...
    });
    
    // Strict = no raw sockets, DNS only to resolver
    assert\!(\!config.allow_raw_sockets);
    assert_eq\!(config.dns_servers, vec\!["127.0.0.53"]);
}
```

### Agent API
- [ ] HTTP endpoint routing (REST operations)
- [ ] WebSocket handling (streaming subscriptions)
- [ ] Request validation (schema verification)
- [ ] Response formatting (canonical JSON)
- [ ] Error handling (structured FCP errors)
- [ ] Rate limiting (per-principal)

### Connector Registry
- [ ] Manifest parsing (TOML, embedded)
- [ ] Signature verification (Ed25519 over manifest hash)
- [ ] Version comparison (semver)
- [ ] Discovery (local, registry)
- [ ] Content-addressed storage (blake3 hash)

## Mocking Strategy
- Mock connector processes (no real subprocess)
- Mock IPC communications (channel-based)
- Inject various failure modes (crash, timeout, malformed)
- MockClock for deterministic timeout testing

## Test File Organization
```
crates/fcp-host/tests/
├── supervisor_tests.rs
├── ipc_tests.rs
├── zone_enforcer_tests.rs
├── capability_tests.rs
├── sandbox_tests.rs
├── agent_api_tests.rs
├── registry_tests.rs
└── vectors/
    └── host/
        ├── supervisor_restart_policy.json
        ├── capability_verification.json
        ├── sandbox_configs.json
        └── ipc_scenarios.json
```

## Logging Requirements
All tests MUST emit structured JSON:
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_supervisor_restart_policy",
  "module": "fcp-host",
  "phase": "execute|assert|cleanup",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "component": "supervisor|ipc|zone|capability|sandbox|api|registry",
  "operation": "spawn|terminate|verify|configure",
  "connector_id": "test-connector",
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "duration_ms": 5
}
```

### For Capability Tests
```json
{
  "token_id": "...",
  "validation_result": "valid|invalid",
  "error_code": "FCP-2102",
  "operations_allowed": ["invoke"],
  "constraints_enforced": true
}
```

### For Supervisor Tests
```json
{
  "connector_id": "...",
  "event": "spawn|crash|restart|terminate",
  "restart_count": 2,
  "restart_delay_ms": 200,
  "reason": "health_check_failed"
}
```

## CI Integration
```bash
cargo test -p fcp-host -- --nocapture --test-threads=4
```

## Coverage Target: >90%

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI (no flake due to timing/network)
- Uses mocks only (no real external network or subprocesses)
- Emits structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35` with stable `test_name` identifiers
- All test scenarios have corresponding golden vectors

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-t77","title":"fcp.jira: Comment and attachment handling","description":"# fcp.jira: Comment and attachment handling (FCP2)

## Goal
Add first-class support for Jira issue **comments** and **attachments**, including safe upload/download flows that preserve FCP2 security invariants.

Comments and attachments are high-leverage for real workflows, but they also carry:
- PII risk
- large binary payload risk
- data exfil risk

## Scope
### Comments
- List comments for an issue (pagination).
- Create/update/delete comments (with visibility rules when supported).
- Convert between:
  - Jira Cloud ADF (Atlassian Document Format)
  - a canonical connector representation (e.g., markdown + structured spans)

### Attachments
- List attachments for an issue (metadata only).
- Download attachment content (streamed; size limits; hash).
- Upload attachment (streamed; content-type allowlist; filename sanitization).
- Delete attachment (dangerous; approval-gated).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `jira.comments.read`
- `jira.comments.write`
- `jira.attachments.read`
- `jira.attachments.write` (dangerous: data egress into Jira)
- `jira.attachments.delete` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to Jira base hostname and any attachment host if distinct.
- No secrets in logs.
- **Binary safety**:
  - enforce max download size; abort early
  - enforce max upload size; abort early
  - compute content hash for receipts/audit evidence
- **Taint model**:
  - comment bodies and attachment filenames are tainted inputs
  - do not allow tainted text to trigger dangerous operations without approval

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `jira.comment.list`
  - `jira.comment.create`
  - `jira.comment.update`
  - `jira.comment.delete`
  - `jira.attachment.list`
  - `jira.attachment.download`
  - `jira.attachment.upload`
  - `jira.attachment.delete`
- Streaming download/upload helpers compatible with sandboxed IO.

## Test strategy (MUST)
### Unit/integration (mock-only)
- ADF ↔ markdown conversion golden tests.
- Upload tests:
  - filename normalization
  - content-type allowlist
  - size limits
- Download tests:
  - streaming behavior
  - early abort on oversized content
  - hash correctness

### Compliance
- Any write/delete emits OperationReceipt + AuditEvent with:
  - issue key
  - comment id / attachment id
  - content hash (never raw content)

## Acceptance criteria
- Comments round-trip through conversion without losing critical structure.
- Attachment IO is streaming + bounded (no unbounded buffering).
- Dangerous operations are capability + (policy) approval gated and audited.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-t9nj","title":"FIX: connector_target_from_env_matches_runtime test arch normalization","description":"## Bug Summary

The test `connector_target_from_env_matches_runtime` was failing because it compared `ConnectorTarget::from_env().arch` directly with `std::env::consts::ARCH`.

However, `ConnectorTarget::from_env()` intentionally normalizes architecture names for OCI/Docker compatibility:
- `x86_64` → `amd64`
- `aarch64` → `arm64`

## Fix

Updated the test to expect the normalized arch value by using the same mapping logic that `from_env()` uses.

## Files Changed

- `crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs`: Fixed test assertion to account for arch normalization

## Verification

- All 48 fcp-registry tests pass
- Full workspace tests pass
- No clippy warnings introduced","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T06:29:43.400900968Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T06:29:59.155461943Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T06:29:59.155461943Z","close_reason":"Closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["registry","testing"]}
